description_long: This object consists of several scattered, partially wilted flower blossoms and fragments, each displaying a mix of yellow, green, and hints of deep red or purple. The petals are irregularly shaped, with some showing signs of curling and browning at the edges, suggesting they are no longer fresh. The green portions appear to be remnants of sepals or calyxes, and there are no visible intact stems connecting the pieces. The flowers are loosely grouped, with gaps of empty space between the clusters, and some petals overlap while others are isolated. The coloration is uneven, with the majority of petals being pale yellow, accented by green and a single prominent maroon-red section. The overall appearance is that of flower debris or discarded blossoms, possibly from a pansy or similar ornamental plant, scattered on a flat surface.
description: A scattered collection of wilted yellow and maroon flower fragments with green sepals, loosely grouped and partially overlapping, resembling flower debris.
description_view_0: Several yellow and green flower fragments are visible, with one maroon-red petal cluster standing out in the lower right.
description_view_1: The maroon-red flower fragment is more prominent in the lower left, and the yellow clusters appear more separated.
description_view_2: The fragments are seen in a linear arrangement, emphasizing the gaps and the elongated spread of the pieces.
description_view_3: The yellow and maroon flower fragments are grouped more closely, with the maroon section near the center right.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are irregularly arranged, with some overlapping and others isolated, lacking clear radial or bilateral symmetry. The arrangement is fragmented and scattered, with no visible whorled or spiral pattern.
